title: LAKE WASHINGTON SCHOOL DISTRICT – (2023) Request for a complete electronic copy of all Public Records Requests received by the District including the District Responses for the year 2023

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: LAKE WASHINGTON SCHOOL DISTRICT – (2023) Request for a complete electronic copy of all Public Records Requests received by the District including the District Responses for the year 2023
communications.communication:
LAKE WASHINGTON SCHOOL DISTRICT – (2023) Request for a complete electronic copy of all Public Records Requests received by the District including the District Responses for the year 2023

Via email: sparthemer@lwsd.org

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act [RCW 42.56] I hereby request the following records:

1. I request a complete electronic copy of all Public Records Requests received by your agency in the year 2023 and a complete electronic copy of your agency's Responses to each of these Public Records Requests for the year 2023.

Note: Parents and students seeking GRADES and/or TRANSCRIPTS in the year 2023 can be excluded from this request.

RECORDS INSTALLMENTS - ORDER OF PRODUCTION
If production of responsive records will be completed in multiple installments, please begin by producing records in REVERSE CHRONOLOGICAL FORMAT BY DATE. The most recent public records should be produced first and the oldest records produced last by date.

DEFINITION OF RECORDS AND WRITINGS
RECORDS AND WRITINGS as defined by RCW 42.56.010(4): any handwriting, typewriting, printing, photostating, photographing, and every other means of recording any form of communication or representation including, but not limited to, letters, words, pictures, sounds, or symbols, or combination thereof, and all papers, maps, magnetic or paper tapes, photographic films and prints, motion picture, film and video recordings, magnetic or punched cards, discs, drums, diskettes, sound recordings, and other documents including existing data compilations from which information may be obtained or translated.

PRODUCTION PROCESSING AND DELIVERY OF ELECTRONIC RECORDS

Please provide all records in their native electronic format. Please do not instead create new records by printing electronic files then scanning and printing the newly scanned prints.

Washington State Law is clear that the use of 3rd party “portals” (Such as GOVQA) for a public records request is optional for the requestor. Respectfully I decline. Please provide all records electronically via direct email attachment to this email address.

Please do not register this requestor for, nor direct this requestor to the use of any 3rd party commercial portal including but not limited to FOIAOnline, GovQA, NextRequest, FOIAExpress, JustFOIA FOIADirect, WebForm, or any other online service other than a direct NO PASSWORD REQUIRED, NO REGISTRATION REQUIRED, internet cloud-based download link, or electronic email.

Do not send any portion of this public records request to any 3rd party for-profit corporation such as GOVQA, a Grannicus Corporation or NEXT-REQUEST.

Do not register this request with any for profit portal or 3rd party organization such as GOVQA, a Grannicus Corporation, or NEXT-REQUEST.

PUBLIC RECORDS REQUESTS SUBMITTED VIA EMAIL
*** FORWARD THIS PUBLIC  RECORDS REQUEST TO PUBLIC RECORDS OFFICER ***
RCW 42.56.080 - Identifiable records—Facilities for copying—Availability of public records.

"...Agencies shall honor requests received in person during an agency's normal office hours, or by mail or email, for identifiable public records unless exempted by provisions of this chapter. No official format is required for making a records request; however, agencies may recommend that requestors submit requests using an agency provided form or web page."

This public records request is being made via email. This public records request will be honored and fulfilled by your agency via email as requested or relief will be sought per statute.

NATIVE ELECTRONIC FORMAT

Please provide all identified public records in their NATIVE electronic format, either .pst file or .pdf PORTFOLIO file. Please provide all electronic records in the fewest number of individual files as per native format or use free .zip compression in each installment.

FEES
In the event that there are fees, please inform us of the total charges in advance of fulfilling the request in strict compliance with all provisions of the Washington State Public Records Act.

EXEMPTION LOGS
Please ensure all redactions or exemptions claimed by your agency in the production of responsive records are accompanied by a complete and detailed exemption log noting the valid legal reason for each exemption at each redaction location in the record produced, as well as the specific number of pages if any that your agency redacts or withholds in their entirety. Each redaction should be noted by footnote or by a clear reference to the specific justification for that redaction, and only the minimal exempt portion of any record may be withheld.

COMMERCIAL PURPOSE DECLARATION
The Washington State Public Records Act prohibits the disclosure of “lists of individuals” for a commercial purpose (RCW 42.56.070(8)). I declare this public records request is not being made for any commercial purpose whatsoever. Also, this public records request is not being made for a "list of individuals." This declaration satisfies all requirements of RCW 42.56 regarding prohibitions on lists and commercial purposes. No additional commercial purpose declaration will be completed for this public records request.

PRESERVATION OF PUBLIC RECORDS *** ANTICIPATE LITAGATION ***

WAC 44-14-03005 RETENTION OF PUBLIC RECORDS

The lawful destruction of public records is governed by retention schedules. The unlawful destruction of public records can be a crime. RCW 40.16.010 and 40.16.020. An agency is prohibited from destroying a public record, even if it is about to be lawfully destroyed under a retention schedule, if a public records request has been made for that record. RCW 42.56.100. Additional retention requirements might apply if the records may be relevant to actual or anticipated litigation. The agency is required to retain the record until the record request has been resolved.

Note: An agency can be found to violate the Public Records Act and be subject to the attorneys' fees and penalty provision if it prematurely destroys a requested record after a request is made. See Yacobellis v. City of Bellingham, 55 Wn. App. 706, 780 P.2d 272 (1989). However, it is not a violation of the Public Records Act if a record is destroyed prior to an agency's receipt of a public records request for that record. Bldg. Indus. Ass'n of Wash. v. McCarthy, 152 Wn. App. 720, 218 P.3d 196 (2009); West v. Dep't of
Nat. Res., 163 Wn. App. 238, 258 P.3d 78 (2011). The Public Records Act (chapter 42.56 RCW) and the records retention statutes (chapter 40.14 RCW) are two different laws.

COMMUNICATION VIA EMAIL

Please send all requests for clarification, status updates, and production of responsive electronic documents via direct email reply.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. We look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Good Luck.

communications.subject: Five day letter
communications.communication:
Dear Requestor,
This letter confirms that the District has received your request on today's date. Pursuant to RCW 42.56.040(1)(c) et seq., I acknowledge your request. This letter serves as the response required by the Public Records Act, RCW 42.56.520.

At this time, the District estimates that it will be able to produce these documents within the next six months.

Under the Public Disclosure Act, districts may not charge for locating documents or for inspection. The district will assess a fee of .15 cents per page for any copying. The District will email files to you and does not anticipate a fee.
If you have any questions or concerns, please contact me at 425-936-1147 or via e-mail at publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org.


Jesse Burnham, J.D.
Compliance Investigator
Risk and Safety Services, LWSD
jburnham@lwsd.org<mailto:jburnham@lwsd.org> | (425) 936-1147
[image001.png]


________________________________

DISCLAIMER:
Lake Washington School District Confidentiality Notice: This email message, including any attachments, may contain confidential and privileged information. Any unauthorized review, use, disclosure, or distribution of privileged information, including information protected by Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) or other provision of law, is prohibited. If you are not the intended recipient, please contact the sender by e-mail and destroy all copies of the original message.

communications.communication:
09-16-2024

via email to PublicRecordsRequest@lwsd.org

FORMAL REQUEST FOR REASONABLE ESTIMATE OF TIME AND COSTS  - WAC 44-06-085 RESPONSE TO PUBLIC RECORDS ACT REQUESTS

Hello,

 As per WAC 44-06-085 - Response to Public Records Act requests:

1. Please provide a "reasonable estimate of the time required to respond to the request" and 
2. Please "provide an estimate of copying costs" as per

WAC 44-06-085
Response to Public Records Act requests.

2(b) Reasonable estimate of time or costs. The office will provide an estimate of the time required to respond to the request, and may provide an estimate of copying costs pursuant to a specific request seeking an estimate of cost. If the requestor believes the amount of time or estimated costs stated are not reasonable, the requestor may petition the public records officer for a formal review under WAC 44-06-120.

It has now been over 5 months since the original public records request with no production of any records whatsoever.

PRESERVATION OF PUBLIC RECORDS *** ANTICIPATE LITIGATION ***

RCW 42.56.100 Protection of public records—Public access.

If a public record request is made at a time when such record exists but is scheduled for destruction in the near future, the agency, the office of the secretary of the senate, or the office of the chief clerk of the house of representatives shall retain possession of the record, and may not destroy or erase the record until the request is resolved.

NOTICE OF WRITTEN REQUEST FOR FORMAL REVIEW - PUBLIC RECORDS UNIT OFFICE OF THE ATTORNEY GENERAL

As per  WAC 44-06-120(4) - Review of denials of public records requests, estimates of time, estimates of costs, a request for formal review of this public records act request will be made to the Washington State Attorney General.

Thank you.

communications.subject: PRR-2024-109: installment #1
communications.communication:
Dear Requester, 
  
 Thank you for submitting a public records request. Your request, PRR-2024-109 is being processed in installments: 
  
 I request a complete electronic copy of all Public Records Requests (ALL RECORDS AND WRITINGS) received by your agency in the year 2023 and a complete electronic copy of your agency's Responses (ALL RECORDS AND WRITINGS) to each of these Public Records Requests for the year 2023. 
  
 Attached is installment #1. This public records request was received 10/11/2023, request details below: 
  
 I am a freelance reporter working on a story about the seismic readiness of Washington public schools. I am writing to request a list of the Lake Washington School District buildings that have been seismically retrofitted since 1998, along with the type of retrofit, the year the retrofit was completed, and the seismic performance level that the retrofit was designed to provide (for example: risk reduction, collapse prevention, life safety, or immediate occupancy). In addition, can you please let me know which seismic performance level any newer buildings (constructed after 1998) are designed to provide? Thank you so much. 
  
 Lake Washington School District is still in the process of searching for additional records. The district is currently experiencing an unusually high volume of extensive and complex public records requests. We anticipate being able to send you the second installment by 12/20/2024. 
  
 Thank you for your patience in this regard. 
  
 The District strives to provide the most timely and efficient responses to requests. To assist with that process, please send any future communications, requests and responses to publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org . This information is also available on our website at https://url4102.request.justfoia.com/ls/click?upn=u001.nrfdmL-2B1UhWpB4V2ArB2saf7hzZRTWl6NaNk2Ngw2xI94WCmlOAuubZY2eGJvWNCDbiKW-2FoHs-2Fp1FxW-2Fre9NBQ-3D-3DD1hX_NvUDiELrDjR8mh7Q7NvTdiDSnQ5v1TyneOL-2Bk5n56Bz9Yei-2FDXIe3NIVusw9j-2BSVn5aD6XDCSIpL40MZnKfH9dPV0vcwJdNlzb4zVslGq7T3v1Jy9GijPp-2FtfIIi7sTydTi0Br6fzcKP5cSJpOdD9h83izWwCNDkAGnU4rT-2F3xcN2-2BM7sYWBLdwThoWTy01d-2BX03CvdTHj0sWbdE1byUBLQY3QtUKGyrSfGdpAqpeiPns5-2Bv3SSgr44dfGQNFmjRVWWwPHSf0P2qI-2BNu-2FRfJt6S7P3EjFsJkZYrHMrHKw1wXv3EKOqn5Q1mVUqyQCTud .

Dear Requester,

 I request access to communications, documents, drafts, revisions, feedback, notes, chats, emails and texts related to Redmond High School Science departments development and publication of Lesson Plans and Syllabi for the Physics Courses offered at Redmond High School for the 2022/2023 school year and all communications related to this request. 
  
 Lake Washington School District is still in the process of searching for additional records. The district is currently experiencing an unusually high volume of extensive and complex public records requests. We anticipate being able to send you the fourth installment by 2/6/2025.

communications.subject: Installment #4
communications.communication:
Dear Requester, 
  
 Thank you for submitting a public records request. Your request, PRR-2024-109, is being processed in installments: 
  
 I request a complete electronic copy of all Public Records Requests (ALL RECORDS AND WRITINGS) received by your agency in the year 2023 and a complete electronic copy of your agency's Responses (ALL RECORDS AND WRITINGS) to each of these Public Records Requests for the year 2023. 
  
 Attached is installment #4. This public records request was received February 8, 2023, request details below: 
  
 Requesting facilities report for Blackwell Elementary for portables. Any report on portables from 2016 to 2023. Specifically interested in how often district checks portables conditions, seeing the history of workorders, and any other facilities reports. Too many parents have voiced concern on temp being too cold or too hot. Concern is that there is not an accurate read on temps during the day with current portable without air flow. . Would like to learn what tool is used to track temperatures, and how often it is used as well as the temp reads and reports. Seems current conditions are unsafe for learning during days below 40 and days above 70. 
  
 Document names are "Blackwell Portable Assessment" and "ServiceRequestSummary_638....."
 
 Lake Washington School District is still in the process of searching for additional records. The district is currently experiencing an unusually high volume of extensive and complex public records requests. We anticipate being able to send you the fifth installment by 2/20/2025.

communications.subject: Lake Washington School District, WA. - Records Request PRR-2024-109 Completed
communications.communication:
Dear Requestor, 
  
 Thank you for submitting a public records request. The request Request Number: PRR-2024-109 has been completed. Your request, PRR-2024-109, is now considered closed, as previously communicated due to the installments not being claimed, reviewed, accessed, or arranged for inspection. After 30 days from this notification, the request is considered abandoned. 
  
 In accordance with Washington State law, a public records request may be deemed abandoned if the requestor does not claim, review, access, or arrange for inspection of the requested records within 30 days of being notified of their availability. You can refer to the relevant statute here: https://url4102.request.justfoia.com/ls/click?upn=u001.nrfdmL-2B1UhWpB4V2ArB2sQcoFmn8lnh1asJqVwPIxu-2FfVnXzaGfHVs2dg-2BV3dh0ax1bivbr-2FDiVHDmjdPooL39zlkStNEcPxLyLLpUTBt10-3DQGuQ_NvUDiELrDjR8mh7Q7NvTdiDSnQ5v1TyneOL-2Bk5n56Bz9Yei-2FDXIe3NIVusw9j-2BSVn5aD6XDCSIpL40MZnKfH9dPV0vcwJdNlzb4zVslGq7QFWarwwAumuWtN3oXhdHlLReY8XGJCTA4joXGLw6BJZlqx4AiAFLOTLRrUvbkGq92JJPeY7lhstQ2nxL7m8gTtRCci4qrFyLo7SvbqlB2ow55HVu8tqETLDD9meCvFqhfi1hOjplNBlsvSEsxWbPaRxeh1stKZdTJv9lqstsGExINtMCcrE4vVQ50yud6dMYoNqyjeRL-2B4o8o15H-2F3MT1z

 Lake Washington School District does not intend to further address the request. Please follow up with any questions within 30 days, which is 5/18/2025. The completed date, 4/18/2025, begins the one-year statute of limitations which allows you to seek judicial review on or before 4/18/2026.

Redmond, WA 98052

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #PRR-2024-109
communications.communication:
08-19-2025

to: PublicRecordsRequest@lwsd.org

Defective and/or missing “JUSTFOIA” download Links and continued Failure to Provide Installments of Public Records

Dear Public Records Officer,

This is a formal notice under the Washington State Public Records Act, RCW 42.56, regarding continuing serious deficiencies in your agency’s method of delivering responsive records.

The “JUSTFOIA” download links your office references are either missing or do not work/resolve. This is a complete failure to make the records “available for inspection or copying” as required by RCW 42.56.080 and RCW 42.56.520.

This issue has been repeatedly brought to your attention in prior communications across multiple public records requests. Despite these repeated notices, your office continues to provide the same missing or non-functional “JUSTFOIA” links instead of a direct, functional download method. This pattern shows ongoing noncompliance and an unwillingness to take corrective action.

Please provide, in writing:

A detailed explanation of how and why your office is unable to provide responsive records via a direct, functional download link that does not require third-party portals, registrations, or passwords.

Proof that your office has never provided such a direct download link to any member of the public in the past two years, including:

The date and recipient of each records delivery

The method of delivery used

Whether the delivery included a direct, no-registration, no-password download link

If such direct links have been provided to others in the past two years, please identify each occurrence and explain why the same method is being denied in my case.

The Public Records Act does not permit an agency to impose technical obstacles to access. If your system is incapable of providing functioning download links, you must offer an alternative delivery method that allows immediate access to the records, such as direct email attachments, a password-free cloud link, or physical media.

Please treat this as a request for public records and respond within the timelines required under RCW 42.56. Failure to remedy these delivery issues or to produce the requested explanations and proof may result in seeking judicial relief and penalties under RCW 42.56.550.

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #PRR-2024-109
communications.communication:
Dear Requester,

Thank you for reaching out regarding the difficulty you're experiencing accessing public records through our JustFOIA portal.

We’ve investigated the issue and confirmed directly with JustFOIA that their system is fully operational and not experiencing any downtime or technical disruptions. Based on this, it appears that the VPN you’re using may be interfering with your ability to connect to the portal. Some VPN services route traffic through servers that are flagged or restricted by our system or by JustFOIA’s security protocols, which can result in limited or blocked access.

To help resolve this, we recommend the following steps:

Disconnect from your VPN and try accessing the portal directly through your standard internet connection.

If you require a VPN for privacy or security reasons, try switching to a different server location or protocol within your VPN settings.

Clear your browser cache or attempt access using a different browser or device.

If these steps do not resolve the issue, we’re happy to offer an alternative method of access. We can mail you a thumb drive containing the requested records. If this option works for you, please reply with your preferred mailing address and we’ll prepare the materials accordingly.

We appreciate your patience and look forward to assisting you further.

Public Records Office
15212 NE 95th Street
Redmond, Washington 98052
publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org<mailto:publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org>

communications.subject: Lake Washington School DIstrict, WA. Fee issued notification for PRR-2024-109
communications.communication:
Dear Requester, 
  
 This email concerns the public records request, Request Number: PRR-2024-109 , you submitted that was received by Lake Washington School District on Wednesday, April 10, 2024. A fee has been issued for your request for records. 
  
 Following the submission of your request, a preliminary search was completed. The estimated volume of records and associated costs are detailed in the invoice provided. District policy requires that you be provided with a summary of applicable anticipated charges before charges are imposed. This is to allow the requestor to revise the request in an effort to reduce the applicable charges. To this end, a detailed invoice is provided separately for your review. Please click here to view: Request Number: PRR-2024-109 
  
 Please be advised that District policy/procedure [https://url4102.request.justfoia.com/ls/click?upn=u001.nrfdmL-2B1UhWpB4V2ArB2sdDT6VWnYPCfrNw8GE28dNLFru-2BD5tK9Zn73Cfsmpb5xfQNNDYtr1dMm5vktmeCj0iSrGxGsTFS4GDzVEhwn74I-3DnWx__NvUDiELrDjR8mh7Q7NvTdiDSnQ5v1TyneOL-2Bk5n56Bz9Yei-2FDXIe3NIVusw9j-2BSVn5aD6XDCSIpL40MZnKfH9dPV0vcwJdNlzb4zVslGq7SpEYExsOp33LBYRvU4kzSzfzLCIJCzRGtvB5UzIwKaFEGY7bU3OFzSCAPgdWvyNdeeKMeWfcZuc6YNc0-2BOGwOITQYvcTS1-2BqWfBCQ4iyFZKo5BSIv0A1Gefq1CjKBAg6oR7kq0QTTb6C77uS13qrcwRSb1kRL1hSkkChd3AKvARMr3fSBKbyuOkU5wuTLFCJ6nsIRYPexqtd4nfgFYFN6g ] contains the following schedule of charges that may apply to your request: 
  
 1. Ten cents per page for public records scanned into an electronic format or for the use of District equipment to scan the records;
2. Five cents per each four electronic files or attachments uploaded to email, cloud-based data storage service, or other means of electronic delivery;
3. Ten cents per gigabyte for the transmission of public records in an electronic format or for the use of District equipment to send the records electronically; and
4. The actual cost of any digital storage media or device provided by the District, the actual cost of any container or envelope used to mail the copies to the requestor, and the actual postage or delivery charge. 
  
 Alternatively, if the District determines and documents that the fees allowed under this procedure are clearly equal to, or less, than two dollars, the District may instead charge a flat fee of two dollars to provide the records. If the District charges a flat fee for the first installment, the District will not charge an additional flat fee or a per page fee for any subsequent installments. 
  
 Due to the volume of records, the District is exercising its discretion under District procedure and RCW 42.56.120(4) to require a deposit of ten percent of the estimated charges associated with this request.  
  
 After payment is received, we will be authorized by District procedure and the Public Records Act to begin reviewing the responsive records. At that point, we will provide you with the anticipated date of an initial installment. 
  
 Please be advised that we are required to allow you a 30-day time frame to make the pre-payment as outlined above, measured from the date of this email. A failure to receive such payment from a requester within that time frame permits us to consider their request abandoned and close it. Also, the District would also be amenable to narrowing the scope of your request in an effort to reduce the associated fees the District intends to assess. 
  
 Please let me know if you have any questions or concerns about the foregoing or any aspect of the District's work to fulfill your request. 
  
 To view your request status, please click Request Number: PRR-2024-109 
  
 

Thank you, 
  
 Public Records Office 
 15212 NE 95th Street
Redmond, WA 98052 
 publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #PRR-2024-109
communications.communication:
Dear Requester,

Thank you for your message. I’d like to assist you, but your email did not include complete sentences, specific questions, or what public records request you are referring to. I am unable to ascertain what clarification you’re seeking.

Please note that the invoice you received is already itemized. It includes a breakdown of applicable fees in accordance with RCW 42.56.120, such as scanning, electronic delivery, and media costs.

You have indicated a preference not to utilize the JustFOIA portal. As an alternative, we can provide the responsive records to you on a USB drive, which may be sent to you via certified Certified Mail with Return Receipt (CMRR) or picked up in-person at our office. If you would like to receive the USB drive via CMRR, please provide your full name and mailing address. If you would like to pick up the USB drive in our office, please arrange a date and time.

If you elect to receive a USB drive, you are responsible for the actual hardware cost. If you elect to receive a USB drive via CMRR, you are responsible for the actual hardware and mailing cost.  If you choose either of these options, we will send you an updated invoice.

 

Sincerely,

Public Records Office

Lake Washington School District

publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org<mailto:publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org>

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #PRR-2024-109
communications.communication:
October 01, 2025

Public Records Officer
Lake Washington School District No. 414
16250 NE 74th Street
Redmond, WA 98052

via email to PublicRecordsRequest@lwsd.org

Re: Request for Clarification - Itemization of Fee Calculation – Missing Links to productions installments (if any) - Public Records Act (RCW 42.56)

Dear Public Records Officer,

Pursuant to RCW 42.56.120(2)(f), agencies must provide “an itemized statement of the costs” before imposing fees, so that the requestor may meaningfully evaluate, clarify, or narrow the request. Your dated agency invoice must satisfy this requirement and disclose the basis for per-file or per-gigabyte charges.

Additionally, pursuant to RCW 42.56 and applicable case law requiring agencies to conduct an adequate and reasonably calculated search (Neighborhood Alliance v. Spokane County, 172 Wn.2d 702 (2011)), the agency must provide a detailed description of the digital location and digital container of all responsive electronic records.

This includes, but is not limited to:

The system or platform in which the records are stored (e.g., Microsoft Exchange, Office 365, SharePoint, GovQA, JustFOIA, local file servers, network drives, or cloud storage).

The specific digital container where the records reside (e.g., individual Outlook .pst archive, Exchange mailbox, folder structure, database repository, or compressed .zip archive).

The path or directory structure used by the agency to locate the records (e.g., \\Server\PublicRecords\EmailArchives\CustodianName.pst).

Identification of the custodian(s) or account(s) associated with each container.

Whether responsive records were located in native format (e.g., .pst, .msg, .eml, .docx, .xlsx, .pdf) or were exported/transformed into another format for production.

Accordingly, I demand that your agency provide a detailed invoice itemization for public records fees  including:

1. A complete electronic copy of your dated agency fee invoice for delivery of electronic copies of public records responsive to this request.
2. A complete list of responsive record file names, organized in the manner maintained by the agency;
3. A detailed description of the location and digital container where the records reside, and the path or directory structure used by the agency to locate the records, as well as the name and description of the system or platform in which the records are stored and whether responsive records were located in native format.
4. The electronic file size of each responsive record;
5. The calculation method used to determine the charges assessed (per-file, per-gigabyte, or otherwise); and
6. Identification of any additional factors or “actual costs” the agency contends justify the fees.

This disclosure is legally necessary to comply with RCW 42.56.120 and RCW 42.56.100 (requiring agencies to provide the “fullest assistance” to requestors). Without the file names and sizes, I cannot verify the accuracy of the agency’s invoice nor determine whether narrowing the request would reduce costs.

Alternative Calculation – Bundled / Compressed Production

Further, I specifically request that your agency provide a separate fee calculation and invoice detailing the total costs if the responsive files were produced in bundled or compressed format (e.g., .zip archives or native .pst file) rather than as unbundled individual files.

This request is grounded in:

•	RCW 42.56.080(2): Records must be provided in the format requested if “reasonably translatable.”

•	WAC 44-14-050(2): Agencies must produce electronic records in the requested format if it is reasonable and feasible.

•	RCW 42.56.100: Agencies must provide the “fullest assistance” to requestors and may not impose artificial barriers or inflated costs.

Bundling into .zip files (or producing in native format) is technologically trivial, reduces storage needs, and directly reduces per-file charges under RCW 42.56.120. Refusing to provide this option while inflating fees by multiplying file counts is inconsistent with the PRA’s requirements and case law (see Yacobellis v. City of Bellingham, 55 Wn. App. 706 (1989)).

If the agency continues to withhold this information, the result is a denial of access under RCW 42.56.550, as the PRA does not authorize the assessment of vague or unsupported fees. Inflated or unsubstantiated charges are unlawful, and penalties may accrue for each day of violation in addition to attorney fees and costs. See Yacobellis v. City of Bellingham, 55 Wn. App. 706 (1989).

Unlawful Charges for Staff Time

If the agency’s invoice includes costs for search, review, or redaction time, those are expressly prohibited.

•	RCW 42.56.120(2)(a): Agencies may not charge for staff time spent on redacting, reviewing, or locating records.

•	WAC 44-14-07003(2): Staff time is nonrecoverable.

Demand: Identify any portion of the invoice reflecting staff time and immediately remove such charges.

Adequacy of Search

Agencies must conduct a search “reasonably calculated to uncover all responsive documents.”

•	Neighborhood Alliance v. Spokane County, 172 Wn.2d 702 (2011).

Demand: Identify custodians searched, search terms used, systems queried (including backups, archives, and third-party portals), and confirm that no records were excluded based on convenience or cost.

Tracking Installments & Production Integrity

Your agency must produce records in proper installments that preserve identity and accessibility. The request explicitly forbade requiring login, passwords, registration, or portal access. It demanded records be delivered by direct email attachments or open cloud links in native format. I have not received any such delivery. Any “security key” or link requiring a portal violates the request’s terms and undermines meaningful access. If the security key or portal is used to limit or condition access (e.g., enforcing a time window to “claim” records), or to otherwise forfeit access, that imposes an impermissible forfeiture condition not authorized by the PRA.

•	RCW 42.56.080(3): Agencies must provide installment responses.

•	Broken links, overwritten installments, or failure to identify installment contents are violations.

Demand: Provide a production log of all installments, with dates, delivery methods, and content description. Provide direct, no-login/no-registration links or email attachments for all records and records productions produced to date, in their native electronic format, consistent with the original request. Provide a detailed installment schedule for remaining records (with dates and record groupings). Finally, provide a complete, PRA-compliant exemption log for all withheld or redacted records, in compliance with RCW 42.56.27(3).

Preservation Notice

Because litigation is anticipated, the agency is on notice of its duty to preserve all responsive records.

Demand: Implement an immediate litigation hold on all responsive data, including metadata, emails, attachments, backups, and internal communications related to this request. Failure to preserve will be treated as spoliation of evidence. Confirm that preservation holds are in place to prevent alteration or destruction of any responsive records pending final resolution.

Reservation of Rights and Judicial Review

Failure to cure the above deficiencies will be deemed a denial of access under RCW 42.56.550. Please be advised:

•	Statutory penalties accrue per day per record unlawfully withheld.

•	Courts have broad discretion to impose penalties where agencies act in bad faith (Yousoufian v. Office of Ron Sims, 168 Wn.2d 444 (2010)).

•	Attorney fees and costs are mandatory upon judicial review under RCW 42.56.550(4).

Abandonment, expiration, or closure  of this public records request prior to full compliance with RCW 42.56.100 and RCW 42.56.520 is unlawful. The Public Records Act expressly requires agencies to assist requestors in clarification, prohibits premature closure, and mandates retention until resolution. Failure to comply will subject the District to statutory penalties and judicial enforcement under RCW 42.56.550.

I request written compliance within seven business days. If the agency fails to cure, I will seek judicial review and pursue all remedies, including statutory penalties, attorney fees, and sanctions for spoliation.

Thank you.

Please provide this information for the first pay period in June of 2023. The District does not track this information and therefore does not have any responsive records. communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #PRR-2024-109
communications.communication:
Request for Clarification and Formal Letter of Objection

Subject: Request for Clarification and Objection to Production Format – Request PRR-2024-119 – Cure Required
To: Public Records Officer Lake Washington School District 
Date: December 18, 2025
Re: Improper "52 Card Pickup" Production of Records for PRR-2024-119

Dear Public Records Officer,

I am writing to formally object to the format of the installments your agency has provided regarding my request for "Public Records Requests received by your agency in the year 2023 and... your agency's Responses to each."

The Defects in Production The District has provided a "response letter" listing various request descriptions (e.g., Request Date 2/28/2023 regarding janitorial supplies, Request Date 8/12/2023 regarding safety net staffing) and a separate, unorganized "dump" of attachment files (e.g., PO 1000652825 Waxie.pdf, RE_ LWSD_LWEA...pdf).

This production method—often referred to in litigation as "52 Card Pickup"—severs the parent-child relationship between the Request/Response and the Responsive Documents.

By separating the documents from the specific request they belong to, the District has failed to produce the "Response" as a complete record. A "Response" consists of the transmittal message linked to the documents provided. The current production forces the requestor to guess which loose attachment corresponds to which request description.

Violations of the Public Records Act

1.	Failure to Provide Fullest Assistance (RCW 42.56.100): Providing unassociated, disorganized files that require the requestor to reconstruct the agency's data structure violates the statutory duty to provide fullest assistance.
2.	Alteration of Records: The original "Responses" sent in 2023 were likely emails or portal clusters containing specific attachments. By stripping these attachments out and presenting them as loose files, the District has altered the native format and integrity of the records requested.

Demand for Cure and Order of Production

I demand that you cure the defects in these installments immediately. My original request explicitly stated: "I request a complete electronic copy of all Public Records Requests received by your agency... and a complete electronic copy of your agency's Responses to each".

The current production—a loose "dump" of unassociated files—fails to provide a "complete electronic copy" of a Response. A "Response" is a transaction consisting of a specific inquiry linked to specific provided documents. By stripping the documents (such as the Waxie Purchase Order or the Safety Net emails ) away from the context of the request they satisfy, you have altered the record and failed to produce it in accordance with the clear terms of my request.

To cure this violation, please re-produce this installment (and organize all future installments) in a format that maintains the strict association between the Request/Response and the specific documents provided for that transaction.

Required Format: Organized Folder Structure To comply with the request for "complete electronic copies" of these transactions, you must utilize a folder structure that preserves the integrity of the records:

Folder Naming: Create a unique digital folder for each distinct Public Records Request received (e.g., a folder named "PRR-2023-02-28-Janitorial" or "PRR-2023-08-12-SafetyNet").

Folder Contents: Within each folder, you must include:

The Original Request: A digital copy of the actual request received by the agency (e.g., the email or portal entry from the citizen), as explicitly demanded in Item 1 of my request.

The Responsive Records: The specific files provided to that citizen (e.g., the Waxie PO must be located inside the "Janitorial" folder; the Kelly Pease emails  must be located inside the "Safety Net" folder).

The Agency Response: The cover letter or email sent by the agency to the citizen.

This structure is the only way to fulfill the request for "Responses"  rather than a disorganized collection of raw data.

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #PRR-2024-109
communications.communication:
Dear Requester,

Thank you for your email regarding request PRR-2024-109. Your original request stated:

1. I request a complete electronic copy of all Public Records Requests received by your agency in the year 2023 and a complete electronic copy of your agency's Responses to each of these Public Records Requests for the year 2023.

Note: Parents and students seeking GRADES and/or TRANSCRIPTS in the year 2023 can be excluded from this request.

To fulfill your request, we interpreted “all Public Records Requests received by your agency in the year 2023” to mean the District’s official public records log, which contains the full content of each request and response as maintained by the agency. This log is the authoritative record of all 2023 requests and responses. If you intended to receive the original submissions (such as individual emails or portal entries) instead of the log, please let us know, and we will amend the scope immediately.

We understand your concern about identifying files in the latest installment. Going forward, we will make sure to provide an email with a list of the file names for the installment. Please see the list below for the most recent installment provided 12/15/2025:

Omnia Contract 202239-01.pdf
PO 1000652825 Waxie.pdf
RE_ Special Education Letter to Families of Stu....pdf
RE_ Special Education Letter to Families of Stu...(1).pdf
FW_ Letters for Translating.pdf
Safety Net Programming at FHMS.pdf
RE_ LWSD_LWEA Bargain Team Request....pdf
Letters for Translating.pdf
Mail.pdf
Hoard - Log of Documents.pdf
Accepted_ Safety Net Programming at FHMS(2).pdf
Accepted_ Safety Net Programming at FHMS(1).pdf

No records have been altered. They are provided as maintained in the District’s system and attachments are preserved in their original format.

If you prefer the records in a bundled zip file or in native email format please let us know. Currently, JustFOIA does not support folder organization but we can offer alternatives such as bundled installments. Please note, if any exemptions or redactions are applied, they will be detailed in an exemption log within the installment.

To ensure we meet your expectations please advise if you would prefer all records delivered at once in a single zip file after the district completes its searches, reviews, and redactions of if the District should continue with installments as records are located.

Please let us know your preferred format and delivery method so we can proceed accordingly. Thank you for your email; we look forward to hearing from you. Please reach out with any additional questions.

Thank you,

Public Records Office

Lake Washington School District

publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org<mailto:publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org<mailto:publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org%3cmailto:publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org>>

 Please be sure to download and save a copy of the records for your use. title: Public surveillance records request (Texas Department of Public Safety)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Public surveillance records request (Texas Department of Public Safety)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1.	⁠All policies and procedures governing how your agency handles public records requests related to:
	⁠•	⁠Flock Safety systems
	⁠•	⁠License Plate Reader (LPR) technology
	⁠•	⁠Automatic License Plate Reader (ALPR) systems
	⁠•	⁠Any other license plate recognition or surveillance technology

2.	⁠Any guidelines, instructions, or training materials provided to staff regarding the processing of public records requests for the above-mentioned technologies.

3.	⁠Any written procedures for redacting, exempting, or withholding records related to license plate reader systems.

If any portion of this request is denied, please provide a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption for each denial.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel Derfus

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (St. Johns County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (St. Johns County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2201921/ Thank you for contacting the Records Unit of the St. Johns County Sheriff’s Office. 
 
We are in receipt of your records request. 
 
You can expect an email response from our unit representative, as email is our primary means of contact. 
Response times may vary for requests submitted outside of regular business hours, on weekends, or holidays. 
 
We want to inform you in advance that our public records request fees will change effective Thursday , January 1st. 
Please review the attached fee schedule: Public Records fees 
 
St. Johns County Background Check: $10 
 
Crash Reports $10 (Free for involved parties only) 
 
A service charge will apply for public records request assistance that exceeds 15 minutes. 
 
This “shall be based on the cost incurred for such extensive use of information technology resources or the labor cost of the personnel providing the service that is actually incurred by the agency or attributable to the agency for the clerical and supervisory assistance required, or both.”  Florida Statute 119.07(4)(d) 
 
For requests requiring more than 15 minutes of personnel time (for locating, redacting, reviewing, and preparing), a special service charge will be applied of $50 for the first hour and $75 each additional hour. 
  
If any of the above factors change your request, you can reply to this email with the modifications you would like to make.

title: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Secretary of State of Kentucky)

communications.subject: Kentucky Open Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Secretary of State of Kentucky)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Kentucky Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any and all memoranda of understanding (MOUs), agreements, contracts, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information, from January 20, 2025 to the present.

2. Any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements.

3. Any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.

This request includes but is not limited to any document described or labeled as a "memorandum of understanding" or contract relating to voter data, voter list maintenance, or voter roll verification.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: Braxton County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Braxton County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Ariana Davidson
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March 12th 2026

Sue Ann Rutherford, County Clerk
300 Main St #109, Sutton, WV 26601

Dear Sue Ann Rutherford,
Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Braxton County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 to March 12th, 2026. 
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 to March 12th, 2026. 
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 to March 12th, 2026. 
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds
If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 

If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 
Sincerely, Ariana Davidson
arianajdavidson@icloud.com

communications.subject: Re: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Braxton County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication: I have attached copies of the three checks we received for the opioid fund.  We have not spent any of the funds.  Any forms or other info you were requesting will  need to be directed to the County Commission.

title: Request on Political Appointees (Consumer Financial Protection Bureau)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request on Political Appointees (Consumer Financial Protection Bureau)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act. I hereby request the following records:

1. A list of any and all political appointments at the agency appointed from January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request.

2. Copies of resumes for any and all political appointees hired since January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request. These records are likely in the possession of the Designated Agency Ethics Official (DAEO).

Please provide these records as available on a rolling basis.

Documented is an investigative watchdog and reporting group that explores the relationship between special interests and government. This request is likely to contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of the operations of the government. Disclosure of the requested records serves the public interest, and this request is not being made for any commercial purposes. Therefore, I am requesting a waiver of any fees. If you do not grant my fee waiver request, please notify me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request be filled electronically by email attachment or other electronic file transfer if available, or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter.  I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,
Nina

title: Face recognition agreements, training, and use materials (Tulsa Police Department)

communications.subject: Oklahoma Open Records Act Request: Face recognition agreements, training, and use materials (Tulsa Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a public records request being submitted under Zackary DeCicco.

I hereby request the following records related to this agency’s use of any face recognition platform, including but not limited to any system over which this agency does not maintain primary ownership but to which members of the agency have access or any platform that enables face recognition:

1. AGREEMENTS and CONTRACTS
Any and all agreements (including related amendments, attachments, and exhibits) related to the acquisition and use of this "software," algorithm, or program.

2. INSTRUCTIONAL MATERIALS and TRAINING
All instructional materials, presentations and presentation materials and other guidance on the use of "the software." Responsive materials include recorded video and audio, PowerPoint files, prepared remarks, and slides. This also includes training material governing the use, sharing, or access to the "software" or any data related to or collected by the face recognition software/technology, including the legal standard that is required before using the technology.

3. PRIVACY ASSESSMENTS
A copy of any data retention guidelines or policies or other materials evaluating or guiding the security of the "software" or the privacy of the data involved.

4. USE POLICIES 
Please provide a copy of any policy directives, guidance documents, memoranda, training materials, or similar records governing the use and function of this technology for any law enforcement purpose. Responsive materials include all those related to data retention, permissible and impermissible use, and security standards.

5. USE LOGS
All logs documenting the use of and searched conducted using the face recognition system for the period from August 1, 2025 through February 23, 2026. 

This is a request being made in the public interest and not for commercial motives.

If your office anticipates that it will need to charge fees for the processing of this request, please inform me of the estimated costs to fulfill this request in advance of fully processing this request.

Please provide these materials to me electronically, if possible.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response in accordance with the dictates of the public records law.

Sincerely,

Zackary DeCicco

title: Joseph Alfred McNeil (March 25, 1942 – September 4, 2025)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Joseph Alfred McNeil (March 25, 1942 – September 4, 2025)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into civil rights activist and Air Force Maj. Gen. Joseph Alfred McNeil (March 25, 1942 – September 4, 2025). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2025/09/05/us/joseph-mcneil-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an assistant editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

you receive a comment that your FOIPA request number was not located in the
database, please check back at a later date.

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Short Description of Request: Requesting the incident report from Sparks Police Department
My son Nathan A. Bronfman (34yrs) passed away and was found in his car in Sparks this past November. I am requesting the police report regarding the investigation and findings.

Nathan Adriaan Bronfman (deceased)
Born: May 12th, 1991
Nationality: US and Netherlands
Location: Outside of 6155 Telehurst Dr. Sparks, NV 89434
Police Case Number: 25-8127
Date: 11/8/2025
Time of 911 call: 1810

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Scott T. Bronfman

communications.subject: Your Request 20260789 - 25-8127 | REQUEST FOR REPORT NATHAN A. BRONFMAN DECEASED NOV. 8, 2025 is completed
communications.communication:
Good day, 
 
Your request: 20260789 - 25-8127 | REQUEST FOR REPORT NATHAN A. BRONFMAN DECEASED NOV. 8, 2025 is now completed. 
 
 The record(s) you have been provided do not contain any documentation from other agencies or any confidential information.  Sparks Police Department  is required to redact or remove all personal identifying information (NRS 603A.040), proprietary information (NRS 333.335), information from the National Crime Information Center (28 CFR § 50.12(b); USC 28 § 534(f)(1); CJIS Security Policy Section 4.2.3), information about ongoing criminal investigations (NRS 199.520; Donrey of Nevada v. Bradshaw, 106 Nev. 630, 798 P.2d 144 (1990) ), information about victims of crimes (Nevada Constitution, Article 1, Section 23; NRS 178.5691); and information relating to individuals’ private interests such that disclosure to the public would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of privacy (Clark Cnty. Sch. Dist. v. Las Vegas Review-Journal, 134 Nev. 700, 429 P.3d 313 (2018)). 
 
 If your request contained any of the above information, it has been redacted or removed as required by law. 
 
Electronic records are available here for the next 60 days: 
https://app.sharebase.com/#/folder/145292/share/242-iajmx6Jsno-lISka8-x6g4kpNF4 
 
If you receive “Invalid Source” when attempting to view a document, download the document and open it in a separate window. 
 
For non-electronic records, please refer to the attached Record Summary to schedule an appointment to review the records. 
 
Best regards. 
 
This is an automated email, please do not reply.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Gloucester County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: New Jersey Open Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Gloucester County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Jersey Open Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all books and other printed publications that are banned or otherwise not permitted in prisons. Please include the title and author of the publication. If available, please include the reasons for prohibiting the publication, as well as the date of the decision.

If possible, please send this information in a searchable spreadsheet format, such as an Excel file or a CSV file.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

communications.subject: RE: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Banned books list (Department of Corrections)
communications.communication:
Good morning,

We are happy to announce the ODOC has upgraded to an online public records request portal, GovQA, that is now our central location to process public records requests through.  Therefore, we are no longer processing records requests that come in through resource inboxes (such as the DOC General Public Record Requests).

We kindly ask that you use the following link to create a GovQA account and submit your request.  This new portal will streamline the processing of records.

Please see the following link to GovQA to the "Submit a Records Request" area Public Records Requests | Oregon Department of Corrections<https://oregondoc.govqa.us/WEBAPP/_rs/(S(erfwg1qlil3uvveemnxer342))/supporthome.aspx>, or more general information can be found in my signature line.

Sincerely,

[cid:image001.jpg@01DC91BA.3F4567D0]
Laura Sterling
Public Records Analyst
Legal Affairs Unit
3723 Fairview Industrial Dr. SE, Suite 200
Salem, OR 97310
Cell: 971-375-5381
www.oregon.gov/DOC

The Oregon Department of Corrections has transitioned to an online public records request portal, GovQA. Please visit the ODOC website for more information: Department of Corrections : Public Record Requests : Research and Information Requests : State of Oregon<https://www.oregon.gov/doc/research-and-requests/Pages/public-records.aspx>

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Iowa Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Internal evaluation or pilot assessment reports that summarize performance, adoption, risks, or “lessons learned” from Iowa’s mDL program (Iowa MiD – IDEMIA, Samsung Wallet) between January 1, 2023 and the present.

Agreements, contracts, or memoranda of understanding with vendors or partners, including but not limited to IDEMIA, Samsung, AAMVA, or other providers.

Interagency or interstate coordination documents, including but not limited to MOUs or correspondence with AAMVA or other states regarding mDL interoperability, standards development, or airport integration at Des Moines International Airport and Eastern Iowa Airport.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rebecca Williams

communications.subject: Fees and Scope for Iowa DOT mDL request
communications.communication:
Good morning Ms. Williams,
Your request for information regarding the Iowa Mobile ID program was forwarded to me so I can assist you in obtaining the details you're seeking.  Upon initial review, it appears your request covers a broad range of information spanning multiple years. As the Iowa DOT is subject to state open records laws as well as state and federal Driver's Privacy Protection Act (DPPA) laws, all requested materials must be carefully gathered and reviewed to ensure legal compliance. Please note that fulfilling open records requests can be time-consuming. The DOT provides the first three hours of work on a data request at no charge. After that, a preparation fee of $33 per hour will apply, and this fee must be paid in advance.  To help minimize costs and expedite the process, I wanted to check in and see if there are specific pieces of information you're most interested in. Narrowing the scope of your request could significantly reduce the time and cost involved.  My team and I are here to assist you should you like to talk through what information might be most helpful to you.
Kind regards,

Angel Robinson
Director
Systems and Administration Bureau, MVD
Iowa Department of Transportation
6310 SE Convenience Blvd
Ankeny, IA 50021
515-715-5632 mobile
Angel.Robinson@iowadot.us<mailto:Angel.Robinson@iowadot.us>
Iowadot.gov
[Iowa DOT Logo]

communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Williams,
I hope this message finds you well.
We anticipate being able to provide you with the cumulative fee estimate associated with your open records request by the end of next week. As previously noted in our communication on September 15, 2025, each request includes a preparation fee of $33 per hour, with the first three hours provided at no charge.  Please note that payment of the total estimated fee will be required before we can proceed with the review and release of the requested documents.
If at any point you would like to revise or narrow the scope of your request to potentially reduce the associated costs, I would be happy to assist. Feel free to contact me by email or phone at your convenience.
Kind regards,

communications.subject: RE: Iowa Open Records Law Request: IA mDL Program Records (Jan 2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Good afternoon, Ms. Williams,

We regret the delay in getting back to you and thank you for your understanding.

The Iowa DOT, Motor Vehicle Division, has conducted a thorough search for all relevant materials, including reports, documents, emails, agreements, and communications with both internal and external partners and vendors—such as IDEMIA, Samsung, and AAMVA.

To support your request, we are pleased to waive the fee for the first three hours of document preparation. Based on our initial assessment, we estimate that an additional 1,375 hours will be required to collect, review, and, if necessary, redact the requested materials to ensure compliance with applicable laws and privacy standards.

At the standard rate of $33 per hour for staff time, the estimated cost for this work is $45,375. In accordance with our policy, we kindly request that this amount be paid in advance before we proceed.

Please let us know how you would like to proceed or if you have any questions. We’re happy to assist in narrowing the scope of your request to reduce the time and cost involved.

Warm regards,

Trista Hills
Compliance Officer
Motor Vehicle Division, Systems & Admin Bureau
Iowa Department of Transportation
6310 SE Convenience Blvd
Ankeny, IA 50021

communications.subject: RE: Iowa Open Records Law Request: IA mDL Program Records (Jan 2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Good afternoon Ms. Hills,

Thank you for your detailed response and for waiving the fee for the first three hours of document preparation. I appreciate the time and effort the Iowa DOT, Motor Vehicle Division, has already devoted to my request.

That said, the estimated cost of $45,375 for an additional 1,375 hours of work is prohibitive and would effectively prevent public access to these records. I want to reiterate that my request is made solely in the public interest and not for any commercial purpose. The records relate to the development and operation of digital identity and mobile driver’s license systems, which have significant implications for privacy, civil liberties, and public governance. Access to these materials will meaningfully contribute to public understanding of government operations and emerging identification technologies.

For context, other state agencies have processed similar requests and provided responsive records at no cost beyond any minimal search time. In light of that, I respectfully request that the Iowa DOT waive or substantially reduce the estimated fee so that this important public-interest request can proceed.

Please also note that my request is not withdrawn, and I ask that it remain open while this fee issue is reviewed. I am willing to work with you to identify priority categories of records or an initial subset of materials that would reduce the burden on your staff without undermining the transparency and accountability goals of the request. If you have suggestions on how best to narrow the scope while still capturing the core of the program’s agreements, policies, and vendor relationships, I would welcome your guidance.

Thank you again for your time and consideration, and for your willingness to assist in refining the request. I look forward to your response on whether the fee can be waived or significantly reduced, and on any recommended next steps for a narrower but still meaningful production.

Warm regards,
Rebecca Williams

communications.subject: RE: Iowa Open Records Law Request: IA mDL Program Records (Jan 2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Hello, Ms. Williams,

Thank you for your thoughtful message and for your continued interest in the Iowa Department of Transportation’s Mobile ID program. We appreciate your recognition of the time and effort involved in responding to your request, and we remain committed to working with you to identify a path forward that balances transparency with the practical realities of staff time and resources.

In response to your willingness to narrow the scope of your request, we believe we can significantly reduce the estimated cost by focusing specifically on the release of contracts related to Iowa’s Mobile ID. This narrower scope would still provide meaningful insight into the program’s vendor relationships and operational framework, while substantially reducing the burden on our staff.

Based on this revised scope, we estimate that the time required to review and prepare the responsive materials would be approximately 35 hours, resulting in a total fee of $1,155. This includes the waiver of the first three hours of preparation time, as previously noted.

We hope this adjusted approach aligns with your goals of promoting public understanding and accountability, while also making the request more feasible to fulfill. Please let us know if you would like to proceed with this narrowed scope, or if you have additional refinements in mind.

We value your partnership in this process and welcome your response.

Sincerely,

Trista Hills
Compliance Officer
Motor Vehicle Division, Systems & Admin Bureau
Iowa Department of Transportation
6310 SE Convenience Blvd
Ankeny, IA 50021
[cid:image001.png@01DC5ADF.E6E13D00]

communications.subject: RE: Iowa Open Records Law Request: IA mDL Program Records (Jan 2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Hills,

Thank you for the thoughtful response and for continuing to work with me on this request.

I would like to better understand the basis for the revised estimate of 35 hours for the review and preparation of contracts alone. Because these are finalized procurement records, I want to confirm a few points before proceeding.

Specifically, could you please let me know:

Whether the Mobile ID contracts are already maintained as final, executed documents within a centralized procurement or contract management system;

Whether these contracts are already publicly available, in whole or in part, through the Iowa DOT website, the State of Iowa procurement portal, or another public-facing repository; and

Whether the estimated time reflects substantive legal review beyond standard confirmation of exemptions.

To clarify my request further in an effort to reduce or eliminate review time, I am seeking final, executed Mobile ID–related contracts only, produced as they exist, and I am not requesting redaction of standard contractual terms. If there are limited portions that the Department believes are exempt, I am amenable to those portions being withheld rather than undertaking extensive line-by-line redaction.

If the contracts are already publicly posted, links to those records would fully satisfy my request.

Given the nature of these records as core public procurement documents, I am concerned that a 35-hour estimate may substantially overstate the work required. I would appreciate clarification on whether the scope can be adjusted to allow for production with minimal or no additional staff time.

Thank you again for your engagement, and I look forward to your guidance on how best to proceed.

Sincerely,
Rebecca Williams

communications.subject: RE: Iowa Open Records Law Request: IA mDL Program Records (Jan 2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Good morning Ms. Williams,

We appreciate your continued patience as we work to refine the documentation to include only the materials most relevant to your request. To ensure we have a clear understanding of your needs, we would like to schedule a Teams meeting with you. Please share a few dates and times that work best for your schedule.

Thank you,
Trista

Dear Ms. Williams,

Thank you for your patience. Attached you will find documentation responsive to your inquiry. Please note that, pursuant to Iowa Code 22.7, certain information has been redacted. If you need any additional information, please feel free to reach out. This request has been marked as completed.

Respectfully,

2) All email attachments that contain the term "Flipper Zero" in the name or body (if you are able to conduct attachment searches) [Timeframe: January 2022 through present]

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.
